hero.title: The workspace built for parallel minds.
hero.tagline: Monitor, switch, and resume multiple workstreams from one place
hero.desc: Agents made parallel work easy. Managing it is still on you. Yishan fixes that.
problem.label: Why Yishan
problem.title: Tools were built for serial work. The work is now parallel.
problem.desc: Agents make it easy to spin up concurrent tasks — fixing a bug, refactoring a module, reviewing code — all at once. But each workstream runs in its own terminal, repo, or chat tab. The burden of monitoring, switching, and resuming still falls entirely on you.
solution.label: What Yishan does
solution.title: Give every workstream a living workspace
solution.desc: Yishan turns each task into a persistent, hot workspace. Terminals, agent sessions, file state, and git context stay alive as you move between workstreams — monitor everything from one place, switch instantly, and resume in seconds.
features.label: Core features
features.title: Designed for parallel development
features.0.title: Isolated workspaces
features.0.desc: Each task gets its own branch, terminal, and file state. Parallel work never collides — switch between workstreams without anything leaking across.
features.1.title: Live status across all workstreams
features.1.desc: See every task's state — running, waiting for input, done — in one view. No more opening each terminal to check what's happening.
features.2.title: Leave it running, come back when ready
features.2.desc: Start a task, switch to something more urgent, and return later without paying any setup cost. Every workstream stays exactly as you left it — terminals, agent sessions, files, context — until the work is actually done.
features.3.title: Agent sessions tied to the workspace
features.3.desc: Agent work runs inside the workspace alongside the repo and terminal — not in a detached chat tab that loses context the moment you close it.
features.4.title: Built-in context shared across every workspace
features.4.desc: Each project has a .my-context folder — notes, plans, and handoff details written in one workspace are immediately visible in all others. Switch workstreams and the context comes with you, without dragging it into normal commit flow.
more.label: More ways to work
more.title: Built for how developers actually live
more.desc: Beyond parallel workstreams, Yishan fits the way real development work happens — on the sofa, across a team, and without leaving your workspace to check things.
more.0.title: Voice input
more.0.desc: Dictate prompts, notes, and instructions hands-free. Works while you're away from the keyboard — on the sofa, on a call, or just thinking out loud.
more.1.title: PR status without leaving Yishan
more.1.desc: See pull request state, checks, and review status inline with the workspace. No more jumping to GitHub to find out where a PR stands.
more.2.title: Team collaboration
more.2.desc: Share workspace status and hosts across your team. Everyone sees what's running, what's blocked, and who owns what — without asking in Slack.
more.3.title: Autopilot
more.3.desc: Schedule recurring agent jobs to run on a cadence. Code review passes, health checks, weekly summaries — happening automatically while you focus elsewhere.
roadmap.label: Roadmap
roadmap.title: What's coming next
roadmap.desc: The next phase extends Yishan from your local machine into remote execution, deeper agent control, and mobile.
roadmap.0.title: Remote host workspaces
roadmap.0.desc: Spin up workstreams on a remote machine. Keep your laptop free while long-running agent work continues elsewhere.
roadmap.1.title: CLI + agent workflow integration
roadmap.1.desc: Spawn new workspaces directly from chat or CLI. Center all agent control in one place and let agents trigger new workstreams on their own.
roadmap.2.title: Development lifecycle management
roadmap.2.desc: Move from issue to workspace to PR in one continuous flow without stitching tools together by hand.
roadmap.3.title: Mobile remote control
roadmap.3.desc: Monitor and steer running workspaces or agent tasks from your phone when away from your laptop.
agents.title: Use your preferred agent CLI inside one workspace model
agents.desc: Yishan works alongside the agent tools developers already use, instead of forcing a closed runtime or a brand new workflow.
agents.label: Works with your tools
workflow.label: How it works
workflow.title: From zero to multiple parallel workstreams
workflow.desc: Spin up a workspace for each task, watch them all from one view, and jump back in anywhere — in under a minute.
workflow.0.title: Spin up a new workstream
workflow.0.desc: Create a workspace for each task. Each one gets its own branch, terminal, and agent session — isolated and ready to run.
workflow.1.title: Check in across all of them
workflow.1.desc: One view shows what's running, what's waiting for input, and what just finished. No manual terminal checks.
workflow.2.title: Jump back in anywhere
workflow.2.desc: Pick any workstream and everything is live exactly where you left it — terminal, files, agent session, context.
roadmap.label: Roadmap
roadmap.title: Expanding the workspace model
roadmap.desc: The next phase extends Yishan from your local machine into remote execution, deeper agent control, and mobile.
roadmap.0.title: Remote host workspaces
roadmap.0.desc: Spin up workstreams on a remote machine. Keep your laptop free while long-running agent work continues elsewhere.
roadmap.1.title: CLI + agent workflow integration
roadmap.1.desc: Spawn multiple workspaces directly from chat or CLI. Let agents trigger and manage new workstreams on their own — one command, parallel execution.
roadmap.2.title: Development lifecycle management
roadmap.2.desc: Move from issue to workspace to PR in one continuous flow without stitching tools together by hand.
roadmap.3.title: Mobile remote control
roadmap.3.desc: Monitor and steer running workspaces or agent tasks from your phone when away from your laptop.
cta.label: Get started
cta.title: Run multiple workstreams without the overhead
cta.desc: Let agents work in parallel while you monitor, switch, and resume from one place — each task with its own hot workspace and persistent context.
footer.tagline: Workspace layer for the agentic development era
footer.desc: Built for developers who want to run and monitor multiple agent-powered workstreams without the switching overhead
